instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Surrender Value of ULIP
ULIPs offer a balanced approach to financial planning by combining life insurance protection with market linked investment under a single plan l. A portion of the premium provides life cover, while the remaining amount is invested across market-linked funds to help you work toward long-term financial goals. This dual benefit, along with tax advantages, makes ULIPs suitable for individuals looking for both security and wealth creation.
Despite their long-term benefits, as life’s circumstances change, sometimes you may find it difficult to maintain ULIP. Hence, it is essential to know what you do in case you want to discontinue the policy.
What Is the Surrender Value of a ULIP?
The surrender value of a ULIP is the amount paid to you if you decide to exit the policy before its maturity. ULIPS come with  a mandatory lock-in period of five years. This creates two different outcomes depending on whether the policy is discontinued during or after the lock-in period.
If you decide to surrender ULIP within the lock in period the unit fund value after deducting applicable discontinuance charges gets credited to the discontinuance policy fund where it grows at the minimum guaranteed interest rate, risk cover and rider cover, if any, ceases. The proceeds of the discontinuance policy fund become payable at the end of the lock-in period. .
On the other hand If you choose to surrender the ULIP after the lock-in period no discontinuance charges are levied. You receive the regular premium fund value and the top-up premium fund value (if any) available on the surrender date. Once the surrender value is paid, the policy would terminate.
Ways to Determine the Surrender Value of a ULIP
Before deciding to exit a ULIP, it is important to estimate the amount you are likely to receive. The surrender value depends on policy terms, fund performance, and applicable charges. The following methods can help you assess it accurately.
Reviewing ULIP Policy Documents
Your policy document contains key information such as discontinuance charges, maturity date, and surrender provisions. Checking these details helps you estimate the applicable deductions and the payout structure.
Contacting the Insurance Provider
You can contact your insurance provider to get details about the surrender value of your policy.
Evaluating ULIP Fund Performance
Since ULIP returns are market-linked, the current fund performance and NAV value significantly affect the surrender amount. Reviewing the latest NAV and fund growth helps you assess the likely value at exit.
Consulting a Financial Advisor
A financial advisor can help you evaluate whether or not surrendering the policy is beneficial based on your short-term needs, long-term goals and deductions applicable and assist you in making the right decision.
Factors That Influence the Surrender Value of a ULIP
The amount you receive when surrendering a ULIP depends on a lot of factors. Hence, evaluating these factors is essential before making a decision to surrender the policy.
Policy Duration and Lock-In Period
The number of years the policy has been active plays a key role in determining the surrender value. On surrender during the lock-in period, the unit fund value after deducting applicable discontinuance charges gets credited to the discontinuance policy fund and risk cover and rider cover, if any, ceases. The proceeds of the discontinuance policy fund become payable at the end of the lock-in period. If you surrender after the lock-in period, you can get the surrender value immediately and the policy terminates. .
Fund Performance and Market Conditions
The value of your investment depends on the Net Asset Value (NAV) of the selected market linked funds. Higher NAVs and favourable market conditions increase the fund value, while weak market performance may reduce the NAV and the payout on surrender.
Charges and Deductions Applied
Various charges, such as discontinuance charges, fund management fees, and partial withdrawal charges, may be deducted before the final payout. The applicable deductions depend on the policy terms and the timing of surrender.
Choosing a Suitable  ULIP Plan
Here are the key factors you must evaluate before choosing a ULIP policy
Evaluating Life Cover
When buying a ULIP, check the sum assured that you are getting. It is important to have adequate life cover so that the ULIP can provide financial security to your loved ones in your absence.
Aligning ULIP with Life  Goals
Choose a ULIP that matches your financial objectives, such as retirement planning, wealth creation, or child education.
Assessing Risk Appetite Before Investing
Analysing your risk appetite is crucial to understanding what level of market volatility you can handle without stressing yourself. This ensures you don't make a hasty decision in fear or panic to exit during weak market performance and stay invested for the long term.
Comparing ULIP Plans and Benefits
Before investing, compare different ULIP plans based on charges, fund options, performance, flexibility, and policy features. Choose the one that suits your current financial conditions as well as your future goals.
Key Takeaways
ULIPs combine life insurance and market-linked investment, where the fund value depends on the Net Asset Value (NAV) of the selected funds.
Every ULIP has a mandatory 5-year lock-in period, which affects how and when the surrender amount is paid.
If surrendered before the lock-in period, after deducting discontinuance charges, the amount is moved to a Discontinued Policy Fund and paid at the end of the lock in period.
If surrendered after the lock-in period, the current regular premium fund value based on the prevailing NAV is paid including any top-up premium fund value and the policy would terminate.
The surrender value is influenced by policy duration, market performance, applicable charges, and overall fund growth.
Reviewing policy terms, checking NAV performance, and assessing long-term financial goals can help you make an informed decision before exiting a ULIP.
Conclusion
ULIPs are designed for long-term wealth creation while providing essential life insurance protection. The investment value may fluctuate with market conditions, but the life cover  component ensures financial support for your dependents.
Since surrendering the policy ends both the investment and the life cover, the decision should be taken after carefully reviewing your financial goals and protection needs.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
How is the surrender value of a ULIP calculated after the lock-in period?
The surrender value of ULIP depends on the fund value applicable on the date you make the surrender request. You get the regular premium fund value plus any top-up premium fund value and the policy is terminated.
Can I check my ULIP surrender value online without contacting the insurer?
Yes, you can check the estimated surrender value online through the insurer’s portal or by using an online ULIP calculator.
Are there charges deducted while surrendering a ULIP policy?
Yes, discontinuance charges are deducted only if the policy is surrendered within the first five years of the policy. In case the policy is surrendered after five years, no charges are applicable. .
Is surrendering a ULIP early a good financial decision?
Early surrender may affect the benefit of long-term returns as well as financial protection for your dependents in vulnerable moments, so it is usually better to continue the policy unless there is a strong financial reason to exit.
Disclaimer
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
